page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with a high base clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a substantial 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 210.08 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 98.02 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.99 GB/sec. CPU-centric workloads, such as stress-ng single-core (1881.09) and multi-core (58922.47) tests, perform in the average tier. However, the system exhibits limitations in LLM text generation, yielding a weak 7.8 tokens/sec on medium 7B models and a poor 40.51 tokens/sec on small 135M models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iezn.metal offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, trading off local storage and GPU acceleration for massive RAM capacity and high-speed network throughput. This profile makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory analytics, and heavy caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server is configured with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering 32.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight strong memory bandwidth, with large-block reads reaching 210.08 GB/sec and writes at 98.02 GB/sec, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, LLM text generation performance is weak, making the server less suitable for AI inference. The x2iezn.metal is highly efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications, large-scale databases, and in-memory data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 48 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise databases and large-scale caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs, 1536 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal server delivering massive memory capacity and high clock speeds for demanding enterprise databases and in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs and 4.5 GHz clock speed
Massive 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
High-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for optimized EBS connectivity
Strong memory bandwidth performance exceeding 210 GB/sec for large-block reads
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
